name: Blossom Dearie
abstract: Margrethe Blossom Dearie (April 28, 1924 – February 7, 2009) was an American jazz singer and pianist. She had a distinctive light and girlish voice.

concerts.label: The Jazz Singers: Joe Williams, Anita O'Day, and Blossom Dearie

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: Then I did a couple of records in Paris with Blossom Dearie. She and I had an act. We were roommates for a while in a terrible hotel in New York. We had this act.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Daryl Sherman
about_quotes.evidence: it’s meeting people and bonding in some cases... I got to go to England and started performing at Pizza on the Park in London, which was a place that Blossom Dearie played in
about_quotes.doc_title: Daryl Sherman interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 11, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral
about_quotes.evidence: We’re going over to hear another favorite, Blossom Dearie. Yeah she’s great. We’re going to hear her tonight.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie Cain and Roy Kral interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Ron Carter
about_quotes.evidence: We had some work together; we had some gigs at the Top of the Gate
about_quotes.doc_title: Ron Carter — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
